title: AMD Reports First Quarter 2026 Financial Results
text: AMD reported strong first-quarter 2026 results on May 5, 2026. The company achieved $10.3 billion in revenue, representing 38% year-over-year growth. According to CEO Dr. Lisa Su, 'We delivered an outstanding first quarter, driven by accelerating demand for AI infrastructure.' On a GAAP basis, AMD reported gross margin of 53%, operating income of $1.5 billion, net income of $1.4 billion, and diluted earnings per share of $0.84. Non-GAAP results showed stronger margins at 55% gross margin, $2.5 billion operating income, and $1.37 diluted EPS. The Data Center segment led growth with $5.8 billion in revenue, up 57% year-over-year, driven by strong demand for EPYC processors and Instinct GPUs. The Client and Gaming segment generated $3.6 billion in revenue with 23% year-over-year growth. The Embedded segment contributed $873 million, up 6% annually. For Q2 2026, AMD projects approximately $11.2 billion in revenue (plus or minus $300 million), representing 46% year-over-year growth. Non-GAAP gross margin is expected near 56%. The company announced significant partnerships including expanded deployments with Meta and cloud providers, reflecting strengthening customer engagement for next-generation AI infrastructure products.
